rules.name: ACTIVITYEDGEINCOMING
rules.error_message: Control nodes must have at least one incoming control or object flow.

rules.name: ACTIVITYNAME
rules.error_message: Activities must be named.

rules.name: ACTORNAME
rules.error_message: All Actors must have names.

rules.name: ANNOTATEDELEMENTS
rules.error_message: Problem and rationale elements should annotate at least one model element.

rules.name: ARTIFACTNAME
rules.error_message: All artifact elements must be named.

rules.name: BLOCKNAME
rules.error_message: Blocks must be named.

rules.name: CLASSPROHIBIT-EDIT
rules.error_message: Unstereotyped classes are prohibited; use blocks, activities, or other elements instead.  Classes in packages created by MagicDraw (such as CSV Import) are exempt.

rules.name: COMMENTBODY
rules.error_message: The body of comments, problems, and rationale may not be empty.

rules.name: CONSTRAINTSPECIFICATION
rules.error_message: Constraint specifications may not be empty.

rules.name: CONTROLNODEINCOMING
rules.error_message: Joins and merges must have at least two incoming flows.

rules.name: CONTROLNODEOUTGOING
rules.error_message: Forks and decisions must have at least two outgoing flows.

rules.name: DECISIONNODENAME
rules.error_message: Decision nodes must have a name (this is used to specify the decision).

rules.name: DIAGRAMNAME
rules.error_message: Diagram names may not be blank.

rules.name: ENUMERATIONLITERAL
rules.error_message: Enumerations must own at least one literal.

rules.name: FLOWFINALINCOMING
rules.error_message: All flow final nodes must have one incoming flow.

rules.name: IBDOWNER
rules.error_message: IBDs must be owned by a block.

rules.name: MERGEJOINOUTGOING
rules.error_message: Merge and join nodes must have exactly 1 outgoing flow (object or control).

rules.name: OPERATIONNAME
rules.error_message: Operations must be named.

rules.name: PACKAGENAME
rules.error_message: Packages must be named.

rules.name: SIGNALEVENTSIGNAL
rules.error_message: Signal Events must have a signal defined.

rules.name: SIGNALNAME
rules.error_message: All signals must be named.

rules.name: STMACHINENAME
rules.error_message: State machine names may not be blank.

rules.name: TERMNAME
rules.error_message: Glossary terms must be named.

rules.name: USECASENAME
rules.error_message: Use Cases must be named.

rules.name: VALUENAME
rules.error_message: Value properties must be named.

rules.name: ACTPARTYPE
rules.error_message: All activity parameter nodes must be typed by signals, BLOCKS, or value types.

rules.name: CONNECTOREND
rules.error_message: Connector ends must be proxy ports.

rules.name: CONSTRAINTBLOCKDOC
rules.error_message: All constraint blocks must have documentation.

rules.name: CONSTRAINTTYPE
rules.error_message: Constraint properties must be typed by constraint blocks.

rules.name: CONVEYTYPE-BLOCK
rules.error_message: Item flows may only convey BLOCKS.

rules.name: CONVEYTYPE-SIGNAL
rules.error_message: Item flows may only convey signals.

rules.name: ENTRYEXITNAME
rules.error_message: Entry and exit points for state machines must be named.

rules.name: STMCLASSIFIERBEHAVIOR
rules.error_message: If a block owns one or more state machines, one of those state machine must be the block's classifier behavior.

rules.name: ACCEPTEVENTOUTPUT
rules.error_message: Accept Events triggered by signal events must own an output pin.  If you are modeling a signal that triggers a state transition, associate the object flow with an item flow and realize the transition.

rules.name: MESSAGEFLOWNEEDED
rules.error_message: This message signature is a signal and is not realized by any item flows or flow sets.

rules.name: MESSAGESIGNATURE
rules.error_message: All messages on sequence diagrams must have signatures assigned (signal or operation).  Reply, Create, and Delete Messages are exempt.

rules.name: STMINTEGRITY
rules.error_message: State machines may only call operations owned within their owning block's structural decomposition (owned by blocks typing its parts).

rules.name: SUBMACHINESTR
rules.error_message: Submachine states must reference state machines owned by the owning block or blocks that type part properties within the owning block's structure.
